data.title: Umicore Non-GAAP EPS of €1.20, revenue of €3.56B
data.description:
• Umicore press release (UMICF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €1.20.
  • Revenue of €3.56B (+2.9% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted EBITDA of € 847 million and Adjusted EBITDA margin of 24.0%.
  • ROCE of 15.7%.
  • Total recordable injury rate for own employees of 4.5 per 1 million exposure hours.
  • Cash flow from operations of € 849 million and Free Operating Cash Flow of € 524 million.
  • Net Debt at € 1.4 billion, corresponding to a Net Debt/LTM Adj EBITDA ratio of 1.60x.
  • Proposed gross annual dividend for 2025 of € 0.50 per share, to be paid out in May 2026.

data.title: Anglo American posts $2.3B De Beers impairment amid weak diamond market
data.description:
• London-listed miner Anglo American (AAUKF) (NGLOY) reported a $2.3 billion pre-tax impairment on its De Beers Group in its 2025 results on Friday, reflecting ongoing challenges in the diamond market.
  • Weak demand from China and competition from lab-grown diamonds contributed to a total net loss of $3.7 billion for the group, the company said in a statement. 
  • Anglo, which last year discontinued nickel and steelmaking coal assets that it is seeking to sell, aims to focus on copper and iron ore assets. It demerged its platinum business in May and said it is moving forward with plans to sell De Beers.
  • The miner has reviewed the value of De Beers after the diamonds unit recorded a third consecutive annual drop in production last year.

data.title: Rimini Street outlines 2026 revenue growth target of 4% to 6% led by Agentic AI ERP expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Rimini Street (RMNI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Seth Ravin, Founder, Chairman, CEO & President, reported "solid execution and continued accelerating sales growth adjusted for the Oracle PeopleSoft support and services wind down" and highlighted the launch of Rimini Street's next-generation Agentic AI ERP solutions. He noted, "We closed 19 new client transactions over $1 million in TCV and totaling $58.1 million compared to 22 transactions totaling $51.9 million last year," and added, "We added 73 new logos that included household global and regional brand wins."
  • Ravin positioned Rimini Street as "the bridge between existing traditional ERP software infrastructure and the capability and benefits of modern AI innovation," expecting subscriptions for both Rimini Support and new AI service offerings to drive 2026 growth.
  • The CEO introduced Rimini Agentic UX solutions powered by ServiceNow, stating, "These solutions are already in production and are helping clients achieve significant operational gains, including 50% to 60% faster approvals, 70% to 80% reduction in order cycle times, improved audit readiness and greater than 95% data accuracy."
  • Michael Perica, Executive VP, CFO & Principal Accounting Officer, stated, "Our fourth quarter results reflect solid execution and early signs of momentum, highlighted by record remaining performance obligations, RPO, growing 11.1% year-over-year." He also said, "We ended the year with a strong cash position and a stronger balance sheet."

Outlook

  • Rimini Street is providing first quarter 2026 revenue guidance of $101.5 million to $103.5 million and reiterating full year 2026 guidance for revenue growth in the 4% to 6% range with adjusted EBITDA margins in the 12.5% to 15.5% range. Management maintained, "We expect growing subscriptions for both Rimini Support as our core service offering and our new AI for the real-world service offerings to fuel top and bottom line growth in 2026."
  • The company continues its wind down of Oracle PeopleSoft support and services, aiming to complete this by July 31, 2028.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the fourth quarter and full year 2025 was $109.8 million and $421.5 million, respectively. Excluding support services for PeopleSoft products, revenue decreased by 0.4% for the quarter and increased 1% for the year.
  • Annualized recurring revenue was $411.4 million for the fourth quarter.
  • Revenue retention rate for service subscriptions was approximately 88%, with 86% of subscription revenue noncancelable for at least 12 months.
  • Fourth quarter billings were $171.3 million, full year billings $427.9 million. Excluding PeopleSoft, full year billings increased by 4.2% year-over-year.
  • Gross margin for Q4 and the full year 2025 was 60.4% of revenue. Non-GAAP gross margin was 60.8% for Q4.
  • Net income attributable to shareholders for Q4 was $724,000 or $0.01 per diluted share. Full year non-GAAP net income was $0.23 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $11.5 million, and for the full year $49.8 million.
  • Cash balance at year-end was $120 million.
  • Remaining performance obligations (RPO) were $653 million as of December 31, 2025, compared to $588 million a year ago.

Q&A

  • Brian Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners, questioned the visibility into achieving the 4% to 6% revenue growth target for 2026, with CEO Ravin explaining that "the Q1 numbers will obviously be a component of what was signed in Q4 because these are subscription contracts rolling into Q1." Perica added, "there is the PeopleSoft component in that guidance... when excluding into Q1, the PeopleSoft component, which will be down year-over-year and expected down sequentially... we do expect it to be a growth period."
  • Kinstlinger pressed on what gives improved visibility, with Perica highlighting "the momentum... a few quarters in a row here with our growing TCV, obviously laying down the foundation, giving us a higher base."
  • Kinstlinger also asked about Agentic AI project ramp; Ravin responded, "We've been doing projects already on Agentic AI... Those projects have bringing -- they're bringing in both professional service revenue, which you get right away... And then there are subscription components, which could then take a little longer to ramp."
  • Richard Baldry, ROTH Capital Partners, asked about elevated COGS and sales/marketing spending; Ravin explained, "you're seeing us ramp up a little bit in the sales and marketing because we have new products and services to bring to market... We're in the process of hiring roughly 20 new sellers to get us into the early 90s."
  • Baldry further probed the impact of litigation settlement on pipeline and win rates. Ravin stated, "I think if you look at the fact that we reached a settlement in July of 2025, and you look at the increase in pipes, you look at the increase in win rates... I think you're seeing win rates that are some of the highest we've ever seen."
  • Andrew Sherman, TD Cowen, focused on RPO growth and go-to-market productivity. Perica noted, "No particular trend that altered from last quarter with regard to the constitution on the duration of our RPO."
  • Alex Fuhrman, Lucid Capital Markets, asked if growth will be driven by new clients, retention, or higher spend per customer; Ravin replied, "we have a combination... 87% of revenue comes from support, about 13% from our optimized services. And now we have the entirely new accretive innovation services."
  • Daniel Hibshman, Craig-Hallum, asked about adoption scale for Rimini Agentic UX solutions; Ravin described early adoption as "walk before you jog before you run," with customers "very interested in the path."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was inquisitive and at times pressing, especially on revenue visibility, sales productivity, and the implications of new product launches.
  • Management projected confidence in prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly referencing "momentum," "higher confidence factor," and "confidence entering this year."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive about growth prospects, while analysts remained focused on execution risks and underlying drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw the formal launch and first reported client gains for Agentic AI ERP and Agentic UX solutions, while the previous quarter focused on building use cases and partnerships.
  • Revenue for Q4 2025 was $109.8 million, up from $103.4 million in Q3 2025; annualized recurring revenue also increased from $391 million to $411.4 million.
  • Gross margin improved to 60.4% from 59.9% in Q3.
  • The company reiterated its focus on winding down PeopleSoft support, with its share of revenue decreasing from 5% in Q3 to 4% in Q4.
  • Management sentiment shifted to greater confidence, citing stronger pipelines, improved close rates, and more aggressive sales hiring.
  • Analysts' questions in both quarters revolved around growth drivers, retention, and execution, but the current quarter included more focus on the impact of new AI solutions and operational leverage post-litigation.

Risks and Concerns

  • The wind down of Oracle PeopleSoft support remains a key challenge, with related revenue expected to decrease through July 2028.
  • Management cited higher retention losses in 2025 than expected, impacting revenue growth.
  • Increased sales and marketing expenses and investments in new sellers could pressure margins if not matched by revenue growth.
  • Although litigation costs have declined, ongoing compliance and wind-down costs for PeopleSoft remain.

Final Takeaway

Rimini Street closed 2025 with momentum in sales bookings, the launch of Agentic AI ERP and UX solutions, and a strengthened balance sheet. Management is confident that investments in AI-powered innovation and expanded sales capacity set the stage for 4% to 6% revenue growth in 2026, supported by a robust pipeline and accelerating client adoption. The company remains focused on growing its core support business and scaling new innovation services, while navigating the phased wind down of PeopleSoft support and continuing to drive operational efficiency.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
